reasoning_model_1: The generated audio displays a critical failure in content accuracy and generation control. Instead of reading the sentence once, the model enters a repetitive loop, reciting the script approximately eight times continuously (from 00:00 to the end). This constitutes a major content error (excessive additions). Regarding the paralinguistic core requirement (Volume), there is no discernible gradual increase from quiet to loud within any of the repeated segments; the volume remains generally constant and monotonous. The audio is flagged as a technical failure due to the infinite loop behavior.
reasoning_model_2: The model achieves perfect content accuracy, reading the script exactly as requested without errors. The fluency is high, with a natural cadence. Regarding the paralinguistic core requirement (Volume), the instruction was to start quietly and gradually increase to loudly. The model successfully adhered to the 'start quietly' instruction by adopting a whispering tone at 00:00. However, it failed to perform the dynamic adjustment; the whispering tone (and thus the quiet volume) remains constant throughout the entire utterance (00:00-00:09), never transitioning to a loud voice as requested. While it failed the dynamic change, the audio is usable and respected the initial state constraint.
model_comparison: Model 1 suffers from a catastrophic generation failure, producing a repetitive loop that makes the audio unusable and incorrect regarding content. It also fails to demonstrate the required volume change. Model 2 produces high-quality, fluent speech with perfect content accuracy. While Model 2 fails to execute the dynamic volume transition (staying quiet/whispering the whole time), it correctly interprets the starting condition and generates coherent audio. Model 2 is significantly superior to Model 1, which is a broken generation.
